[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/54/Piperidin.svg/120px-Piperidin.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Piperidin.svg)

[Piperidine](/wiki/Piperidine "Piperidine"), the parent compound of piperidine alkaloids

**Piperidine alkaloids** are naturally occurring [alkaloids](/wiki/Alkaloid "Alkaloid"), which are chemically derived from [piperidine](/wiki/Piperidine "Piperidine").

Alkaloids with a piperidine [functional group](/wiki/Functional_group "Functional group") are widespread and are usually further subdivided according to their occurrence and biogenetic origin. The most well-known representative of the piperidine alkaloids is [piperine](/wiki/Piperine "Piperine"), which is responsible for the pungent taste of ground [black pepper](/wiki/Black_pepper "Black pepper").
